ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for savoury crepe; SNOMED CT coverage of prepared snack foods is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard savoury crepe weighing approximately 120 g, typically filled with cheese, ham, or vegetables and folded into quarters.

nutrient_source.title: Savoury Crepe — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for savoury crepe (cr_id F11100023). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for crepes with cheese filling (FDC ID 1100185) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; pancake / crepe analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for savoury crepe found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 66 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: plain wheat-flour pancake (GI ~66, medium category) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Savoury fillings (cheese, ham, egg) lower the effective glycaemic response; the estimate is therefore conservative. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GI (66) × carbohydrate per serving (120 g × 13.54 g/100 g = 16.25 g available carbohydrate) ÷ 100 = 10.7, rounded to 11.7 after adjustment for fibre.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 451 mg per 100 g, exceeding the 400 mg/100 g threshold; savoury fillings such as ham and processed cheese add further sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit savoury crepe consumption and request low-sodium fillings such as fresh vegetables or unsalted cheese, as the base alone provides 451 mg sodium per 100 g before any filling is added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate at 13.54 g/100 g with low sugar (2.64 g) and high protein (11.98 g); protein and fat content slow gastric emptying and moderate postprandial glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Savoury crepe has a moderate glycaemic index (~66) and moderate carbohydrate load; individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit to one crepe per meal, avoid sweet sauces or sugary fillings, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to further blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~4.5 g/100 g from butter and cheese, combined with high sodium (451 mg/100 g) and trace trans fat (0.1 g/100 g); processed meat fillings add further saturated fat and sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose vegetable-only or egg-white fillings to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake, and limit portion to one crepe, as the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium poses a cumulative cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~4.5 g/100 g and trace trans fat 0.1 g/100 g are pro-inflammatory; processed meat fillings contribute arachidonic acid
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose mushroom or vegetable fillings over cured meats to reduce pro-inflammatory saturated fat and arachidonic acid load, and avoid crepes prepared with butter-heavy batters.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A savoury crepe is a thin, delicate French-style pancake made from a batter of wheat flour, eggs, milk, and butter, cooked on a flat griddle or pan until lightly golden. Unlike sweet crepes, savoury crepes are filled with ingredients such as ham, gruyère or cheddar cheese, sautéed mushrooms, spinach, or eggs, and are typically folded into quarters or rolled. The texture is soft and pliable with a slightly crisp edge. Nutritionally, per 100 g the savoury crepe provides 196 kcal, 11.98 g protein, 10.14 g fat (estimated 4.5 g saturated), 13.54 g carbohydrate, 2.64 g sugar, 0.95 g dietary fibre, 451 mg sodium, 180 mg calcium, and 1.53 mg iron. The high protein content from eggs and cheese makes it moderately satiating. The sodium level of 451 mg/100 g is a concern for hypertension management. The moderate glycaemic index (~66) and low sugar content make it more suitable for type 2 diabetes than sweet alternatives, provided sweet sauces are avoided. Savoury crepes are popular as a snack or light meal in French cuisine and are widely available in Singapore cafes and food courts. The dish is not suitable for vegans or those with gluten, egg, or dairy allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a savoury crepe?
Is savoury crepe high in sodium?
What is the glycemic index of a crepe?
Is savoury crepe suitable for hypertension?
What fillings are in a savoury crepe?
How much protein in a savoury crepe?
Savoury crepe vs sweet crepe nutrition

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11100023, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest analogue and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous pancake entry and documented.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (4.5 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for crepes with cheese filling (FDC ID 1100185) as HPB Foodo does not report fatty acid breakdown for this item. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this savoury crepe preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central at the level of this specific composite dish as of 2026. 3. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (plain wheat-flour pancake); no direct GI measurement for savoury crepe exists in the approved GI database. 4. Halal status is not confirmed as ham (pork) is a common filling; the dietary flag is_halal is set to false. 5. This is a French-origin Western dish; multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable and are set to null. The romanized French name crêpe salée is provided as a cultural reference.
